description: XSS via file upload vectors. When uploaded files are served back to users/admins.

svg_xss.description: SVG files can contain JavaScript. Served with image/svg+xml MIME type, browser executes JS.
svg_xss.payload:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8"?>
<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" onload="alert(document.domain)">
  <circle cx="50" cy="50" r="40"/>
</svg>
svg_xss.conditions: Server must serve file with SVG content-type (or without Content-Type header)

html_as_image.description: Upload HTML file disguised as image. If server serves it as text/html, XSS executes.
html_as_image.technique: Change filename to .png but keep HTML content; some servers use content detection over extension

xss_in_filename.description: Filename reflected in page (e.g., 'Upload successful: FILENAME')

pdf_xss.description: PDF with JavaScript can execute in browser PDF viewer
pdf_xss.note: Modern browsers sandbox PDF JS — reduced impact but worth testing

xml_based.description: SVG/XML variants that execute in older/less strict contexts

polyglot.description: File that is valid image AND valid HTML/JS (bypass content-type detection)
polyglot.technique: GIF89a with appended HTML: GIF header + HTML/JS payload. Parsed as GIF by magic bytes check but executed as HTML by browser.

mime_bypass: Add magic bytes: \xff\xd8\xff before PHP code
content_disposition: Try serving uploaded files with no Content-Disposition header
If admin views uploads inline rather than downloading, XSS can execute
